summary: Backend tests for expanded Member Profile System + Celebrations endpoints. All 11/11 tests passed. Verified registration accepts new optional fields (phone/dob/gender/city/state) and persists them; PATCH /api/profile accepts and stores all 16+ new fields; enum validation returns 422 for invalid club_type, marital_status, gender; /profile/completion reports 16 total fields with correct pct math; /celebrations/me correctly flags is_birthday and is_anniversary (only when married); /celebrations/today lists Achilles under birthdays (today=07-03) and excludes him from anniversaries when marital_status=unmarried; endpoints require auth (401/403).
critical_code_review_comments: PATCH /api/profile silently ignores unknown keys (extra='ignore' via Pydantic default) — fine, but consider explicit 'extra=forbid' to catch typos in client

profile_completion uses field 'team_id' but registration/seed stores 'team' — that field will always count as missing (verify PROFILE_FIELDS alignment with actual stored keys); PATCH also doesn't allow updating team_id/email so max reachable pct may be capped below 100 for regular users, which could block the +50 XP one-time award
server.py has grown to 2296 lines — recommend splitting into routers (profile, auth, celebrations, missions, etc.) for maintainability
seed_data_creation: Created transient TEST_reg_* user via /auth/register; also toggled Achilles marital_status during tests and restored to 'married' at end.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Server date is 2026-07-03 UTC so Achilles (dob=1988-07-03) triggers birthday celebrations. Tests toggle Achilles.marital_status and restore to 'married'. PROFILE_FIELDS list uses 'team_id' but stored user doc uses 'team' — 100% completion is likely unreachable for standard users, worth flagging to main agent.
